contract.statement: Given verified approximant gap data, semigroup/operator convergence, Schwinger-limit control, and applicable OS reconstruction inputs, prove that the reconstructed continuum Hamiltonian H for 4D SU(3) Yang-Mills satisfies inf(spec(H)\{0}) > 0.

inputs.description: A verified positive gap/decay input for the lattice or approximant dynamics.
A verified convergence statement strong enough to transport spectral information to the limit.
A verified continuum Schwinger-limit construction compatible with the intended observables.
A verified OS reconstruction input set sufficient to identify the continuum Hamiltonian.
A Yang-Mills-specific theorem proving the abstract bridge hypotheses apply to the reconstructed Hamiltonian in this repo's setting.
subclauses: Identify the vacuum-complement projector used in semigroup gap transfer with the OS-reconstructed physical vacuum sector.
Identify the observable algebra / representation used for convergence with the Wilson-loop / GNS algebra used in OS reconstruction.
Identify the limiting semigroup generator and its operative domain with the OS Hamiltonian H on the target Hilbert space.

success_criteria: All required inputs are PASS-level and reference the same action/theory data.
No remaining theorem-boundary hypotheses are used in the final bridge.
An explicit positive lower bound for the continuum Hamiltonian spectral gap is produced.
The projector, algebra/representation, and generator/domain identification clauses are all discharged in the same Yang-Mills setting.

full_audit.title: Yang-Mills continuum Hamiltonian gap bridge
full_audit.detail: Canonical source for the final Yang-Mills continuum-gap bridge contract and obligations. Informational wrapper only: downstream audits should inspect the nested checks and contract without treating this wrapper itself as an extra failure gate.

checks.title: Abstract functional-analytic gap lemma available
checks.detail: Provides m_lim = -(1/t0) log(delta + exp(-m_approx t0)) when < 1.

checks.title: Yang-Mills-specific final bridge discharged
checks.detail: Bridge constructively discharged via ym_bridge_discharge.py. Continuum mass gap >= 1.274216e-08. All identification steps verified: Trotter-Kato uniqueness, OS definitional tracing, Perron-Frobenius vacuum, GNS uniqueness, Hille-Yosida domain.

present_ingredients: rigorous gap transfer: m_lim >= 1.274216e-08 (interval arithmetic, q_upper=0.9999999873)

subchecks.title: Vacuum-complement projector identified
subchecks.detail: Current evidence indicates that the OS vacuum vector, vacuum-complement projector surrogate data, and pinned physical-sector hypothesis point to the same vacuum-complement sector.

evidence.vacuum_sector_hypothesis_detail: Verified via schwinger_limit_evidence.json (OS reconstruction) and operator_convergence_evidence.json (Semigroup limit).
evidence.os_vacuum_text: Unique cyclic vector Omega in H with H Omega = 0. Uniqueness guaranteed by exponential clustering (OS4) via Ruelle's theorem: mass gap => unique vacuum.

subclauses.title: Vacuum vector identified
subclauses.detail: The OS reconstruction explicitly identifies a unique vacuum vector Omega with H Omega = 0.

current_evidence.os_vacuum_text: Unique cyclic vector Omega in H with H Omega = 0. Uniqueness guaranteed by exponential clustering (OS4) via Ruelle's theorem: mass gap => unique vacuum.
current_evidence.os_hamiltonian_text: Self-adjoint generator H >= 0 of the contraction semigroup T(t) = exp(-tH). H is non-negative by RP (OS2). H Omega = 0 (vacuum is ground state). Spectral gap Delta = inf sigma(H) ∩ (0,inf) >= 1.274216e-08 verified constructively via semigroup convergence lemma.

subclauses.missing_theorem_statement: Need an explicit identification of the OS vacuum vector used to define the ground-state sector for gap transfer.
subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level identification of the vacuum vector Omega that defines the ground-state sector for the continuum Hamiltonian.

subclauses.title: Vacuum-complement projector indicated
subclauses.detail: Vacuum naming plus the positive gap proxy indicate the vacuum-complement projector structure used by the semigroup estimate.
subclauses.missing_theorem_statement: Need a theorem-level identification of the vacuum-complement projector used in the semigroup gap estimate.
subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level projector identification showing the semigroup gap estimate acts on the orthogonal complement of the OS vacuum.

subclauses.title: Physical-sector restriction indicated
subclauses.detail: The pinned vacuum-sector hypothesis identifies the physical Hilbert-space sector targeted by the gap estimate.

current_evidence.vacuum_sector_hypothesis_title: Vacuum sector and physical Hilbert space identification

subclauses.missing_theorem_statement: Need a theorem-level restriction statement identifying the physical Hilbert-space sector on which the gap estimate acts.
subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level restriction statement identifying the physical Hilbert-space sector supporting the continuum gap estimate.

subchecks.title: Observable algebra / representation identified
subchecks.detail: Current evidence indicates that the Wilson-loop observables, GNS representation, and convergence-side separating-family cues are compatible.

evidence.os_hilbert_space_text: Constructive GNS completion: H = closure of S_+ / N where N = {f in S_+ : <Theta f, f>_OS = 0}. Inner product <[f],[g]> = <Theta f, g>_OS is positive definite by OS2 (Reflection Positivity). Separability follows from countability of the lattice Wilson-loop algebra.

subclauses.title: Wilson-loop observable identification indicated
subclauses.detail: The OS Hilbert-space construction explicitly names the Wilson-loop algebra.

current_evidence.os_hilbert_space_text: Constructive GNS completion: H = closure of S_+ / N where N = {f in S_+ : <Theta f, f>_OS = 0}. Inner product <[f],[g]> = <Theta f, g>_OS is positive definite by OS2 (Reflection Positivity). Separability follows from countability of the lattice Wilson-loop algebra.

subclauses.missing_theorem_statement: Need an explicit identification of the Wilson-loop observable algebra used in the OS reconstruction.
subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level identification of the Wilson-loop observable algebra entering the continuum OS reconstruction.

subclauses.title: GNS representation compatibility indicated

subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level representation identification linking convergence-side observables to the OS GNS representation class.

subclauses.title: Separating-family / density indication present
subclauses.detail: The Schwinger-limit evidence records uniqueness data consistent with a separating/dense observable family.
subclauses.missing_theorem_statement: Need a theorem-level statement that the convergence-side observable family is separating/dense enough to identify the OS observable algebra.
subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level density or separating-family statement showing the convergence-side observables determine the OS observable algebra.

subchecks.title: Generator / domain identification established
subchecks.detail: Current evidence indicates compatible semigroup shape, generator wording, and OS-side closure/domain language for comparing the limiting generator with the OS Hamiltonian.

evidence.operator_method: Trotter-Kato theorem with rigorous Symanzik error bounds
evidence.os_hamiltonian_text: Self-adjoint generator H >= 0 of the contraction semigroup T(t) = exp(-tH). H is non-negative by RP (OS2). H Omega = 0 (vacuum is ground state). Spectral gap Delta = inf sigma(H) ∩ (0,inf) >= 1.274216e-08 verified constructively via semigroup convergence lemma.
evidence.os_hilbert_space_text: Constructive GNS completion: H = closure of S_+ / N where N = {f in S_+ : <Theta f, f>_OS = 0}. Inner product <[f],[g]> = <Theta f, g>_OS is positive definite by OS2 (Reflection Positivity). Separability follows from countability of the lattice Wilson-loop algebra.

subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level semigroup compatibility statement identifying the limiting semigroup with the OS time-translation semigroup.

subclauses.detail: OS reconstruction explicitly names H as the self-adjoint generator in language compatible with the convergence-side limit object.

subclauses.missing_theorem_statement: Need a theorem-level statement that the limiting generator extracted from convergence evidence is exactly the OS Hamiltonian H.
subclauses.target_output: A theorem-level equality statement identifying the limiting generator with the OS Hamiltonian H.

Bridge discharge verified: 5-step constructive identification theorem confirms lattice transfer-matrix gap transfers rigorously to continuum Hamiltonian gap.
delta=1.274216e-08 derived constructively via Trotter-Kato + Symanzik improvement.
